enriched_points.expanded_content: In vitro studies, such as those on Feraheme, highlight effective evaluation of nanotoxicity, demonstrating biological interactions and potential hazards of nanomaterials.
Research has revealed crucial mechanisms of nanotoxicity, enhancing our understanding of how nanomaterials interact with biological systems and influencing safety protocols.
Findings from in vitro assessments have driven the design of safer nanomaterials, prompting alterations in size, surface properties, and functionalization for reduced toxicity.
Future studies aim to integrate AI and predictive modeling to enhance in vitro assessments, improving accuracy and accelerating the development of safer nanotechnology.
